articles.title: ESPN’s Stephen A. Smith the only one who benefits from ongoing feud with Celtics star Jaylen Brown
articles.description:
Smith and Brown's Twitch/ESPN beef continued into this week.

The post ESPN’s Stephen A. Smith the only one who benefits from ongoing feud with Celtics star Jaylen Brown appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Stephen A. Smith's feud with Jaylen Brown continues to attract media attention.
Brown's ongoing conflict with Smith may distract from Celtics' performance this season.
Local fans are divided, impacting community support for Brown and the Celtics.

articles.title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
articles.description:
One NBA insider reported that Kawhi Leonard could be a prime trade candidate this offseason.

The post 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Celtics may pursue Kawhi Leonard as a trade candidate this offseason.
Insider reports suggest multiple players could enhance Boston's roster for 2026.
Potential trades could significantly affect Celtics' competitiveness in the upcoming season.

articles.title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
articles.description:
Division II Bentley University and Division III Endicott College are headed to their respective championship round locations.

The post 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bentley University and Endicott College advance to NCAA World Series championships.
Bentley competes in Division II; Endicott represents Division III in their respective tournaments.
Local fans can support both teams as they aim for national titles this June.

articles.title: Twins beat Red Sox 4-2 despite Boston’s 9th inning comeback attempt
articles.description:
Boston dropped to 2-20 when scoring two runs or fewer.

The post Twins beat Red Sox 4-2 despite Boston’s 9th inning comeback attempt appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Twins defeated Red Sox 4-2 on May 23, 2026.
Boston's record now stands at 2-20 when scoring two runs or fewer.
Residents face disappointment as team struggles to secure wins at home.

articles.title: Suspect dead after opening fire near White House security checkpoint, Secret Service says
articles.description:
The U.S. Secret Service says a man who opened fire near a White House security checkpoint is dead

The post Suspect dead after opening fire near White House security checkpoint, Secret Service says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A man opened fire near a White House security checkpoint and is now dead.
The incident occurred on May 23, 2026, prompting a significant law enforcement response.
Boston residents should remain vigilant as security measures may increase in response.
